SnowDistribution: Good snow cover above 600 meters on leeward aspects. Poor visibility in the morning, followed by a clear and sometimes sunny afternoon.  Winds approx 10mph or less by the afternoon.
Comment: The South-Easterly airflow continues. Freezing level set to lower to glen level Tuesday into Wednesday.
WeatherInfluences: Light overnight South-Easterly winds increasing to gale force during the afternoon. Light snow showers expected during daylight hours. The freezing level initially will be around 1000 meters, lowering to 700 meters by the end of the day.
SnowStability: Moderately bonded windslab will continue to build on South-West through North to North-East aspects above 950 meters. These instabilities are localised and will  affect wind-sheltered locations such as corrie headwalls, gully lines, and the leeward side of ridge crests. On other aspects and lower elevations, the snowpack will continue to consolidate and be generally stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Increasingly light South-Easterly winds. Light showers in the morning cleared to give a dry afternoon with good visibility. The freezing level rose to around 1000 meters.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately bonded windslab is present on South-West through North to North-East aspects above 950 meters. These instabilities are localised affecting wind-sheltered locations such as corrie headwalls, gully lines, and the leeward side of ridge crests.. On other aspects and below 950 meters the snowpack continues to consolidate and is generally stable.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: A dry day with the freezing level around 900m. 20-30mph winds feeling cold.
Comment: Above 900m, many snow slopes that have been exposed to the wind are frozen with heavy rimming in places. Still some water ice on paths at lower elevations
WeatherInfluences: It will be mainly dry but some light snow is possible during the afternoon. The freezing level will remain around 900m with South-Easterly winds gradually strengthening during the day
SnowStability: Above 900m, older areas of well bonded windslab will be present, mainly on steep West, North-West and North aspects. Some localised instabilities will also be present in these locations. Elsewhere, much of the snowpack will maintain a frozen crust. Below 900m the snowpack is gradually thawing.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with a moderate South-Easterly wind over the summits. The freezing level was around 900m.
ObservedSnowStability: Above 900m, older areas of well bonded windslab are present, mainly on steep West, North-West and North aspects. Some localised instabilities are also present in these locations. Elsewhere, much of the snowpack has a frozen crust. Below 900m the snowpack is gradually thawing.  The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Wind scoured on many aspects with deeper accumulations of drifted snow in wind sheltered locations.  Winds approx 10 mph.  No visibility through much of the day above 600 metres.
Comment: Only trace of new snow expected, Icy conditions at higher levels.
WeatherInfluences: Mainly dry conditions with only light snow showers expected later in day. Winds will generally be from the East-South-East, fresh initially becoming strong by evening. The freezing level will be around 1000 metres.
SnowStability: Many slopes exposed to the recent South-Easterly winds will be stable. Older windslab will exist in localised areas such as sheltered gullies and steep slopes with a mainly Westerly to Northerly aspect above 1000 metres. Wind transport of existing snow later in the day may add fresh windslab to these areas, fresh accumulations are not expected to be deep or extensive. Corrie rims and gully tops will be most affected, cornices will be fragile. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light new snow overnight has affected slopes mainly above 800 metres,  Winds have been moderate to fresh mainly from the South-East. The freezing level is around 950 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Much of the snowpack has been scoured by the recent strong South-Easterly wind, stability in these areas is generally good and icy in many places.  Older windslab exists mainly above 950 metres on Westerly and Northerly aspects with some fresh moderately bonded localised accumulations forming overnight. Most affected are gully exits and steep corrie headwalls. Some fragile cornices exist in these areas. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Decent cover above 700 metres, snowpack generally firm.  Cloudy on the tops but good visibility elsewhere.  Winds 10-20 mph on the summits.
Comment: Slip hazard on the firm, wind scoured snow surface at higher levels.
WeatherInfluences: Another generally dry day with strengthening South-Easterly winds. The freezing level will be around 900 metres.
SnowStability: The snowpack will continue to very slowly consolidate. Area of dense and reasonably well bonded windslab will remain on sheltered West to North aspects above 900 metres. On other aspects above 900 metres the snowpack will be firm and icy. At lower levels it will continue to gently thaw. The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a generally dry day with moderate Southerly winds. The freezing level crept up to around 1000 metres during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is continuing to consolidate in the settled conditions. There are some areas of old dense and reasonably well bonded windslab persist mainly in sheltered West to North aspects above 900 metres. On other aspects the snowpack has a firm, icy surface and is well bonded and stable.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Deep snow - hard going on foot. Cloud base 700m, later 1000m. Winds easing to light.
Comment: Mountain conditions deteriorating from late morning. Firm surface laters developing as the freezing level lowers.
WeatherInfluences: Light precipitation arriving before daylight will get heavier from mid-morning. This will initially fall as snow above 850 metres, later falling as snow above 650 metres. The freezing level will lower from 900 metres overnight, reaching 700 metres by the end of the afternoon. Light winds will increase to become strong South-Easterly during the heaviest of the snowfall.
SnowStability: Stability will generally be good with firm surface layers developing above 900 metres, later above 700 metres as the freezing level lowers. Snowfall and drifting deposits will form fresh unstable windslab in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres later in the day. Fragile cornices will start to develop.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light snow showers above 900 metres during the morning, drier later. Moderate, easing to light South-Easterly winds. Freezing level around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: A gently rising freezing level has encouraged consolidation with much snow now demonstrating good stability. In wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 950 metres, deeper accumulations contain moderately bonded layers. Large cornices are present above these aspects. Wind exposed slopes are firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Limited firm snow in specific places. Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Breezy on the tops, 10 - 20mph.
Comment: The Easterly airstream continues with little change expected. Snow will be firm and run outs could have serious consequences.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will be around summit level, dropping to 800 metres towards the end of the forecasting period. Light Easterly winds initially, increasing throughout the daytime to become strong. No precipitation is expected.
SnowStability: No significant change is expected with the snowpack being firm and stable throughout. Greatest snow amounts are predominantly on North-West through North and East to Southerly aspects above 700m. Elsewhere, snow is confined to gullies and patches. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mainly dry day with light to moderate Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm and stable throughout. Greatest snow amounts are predominantly on North-West through North and East to Southerly aspects above 700m. Elsewhere, snow is confined to gullies and patches. The avalanche hazard is Low.
